---
title: "Module Library"
---

# Reusable teaching modules

These pages **follow the same story as the slides**, but you can read them slowly on the website: good for **before class**, **catching up after a missed day**, or **review** before an exam.

We use **two kinds of examples**: **simulated genes** (we wrote the rules, so we know what is true) and **[Palmer Penguins](../dataset-penguins.qmd)** (real measurements you can plot and argue about).

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
## Slide quick links (rendered decks)

Open a section directly after `quarto render`:

| Day | Deck | Example deep link |
|-----|------|-------------------|
| 1 | [day-01-monday.html](../slides/day-01-monday.html) | [#/elastic-net](../slides/day-01-monday.html#/elastic-net), [#/lasso-cv](../slides/day-01-monday.html#/lasso-cv) |
| 2 | [day-02-tuesday.html](../slides/day-02-tuesday.html) | [#/part-c-pipeline](../slides/day-02-tuesday.html#/part-c-pipeline) |
| 4 | [day-04-thursday.html](../slides/day-04-thursday.html) | [#/part-e-metrics](../slides/day-04-thursday.html#/part-e-metrics) |

*Authoring:* edit [`slides/*.qmd`](../slides/day-01-monday.qmd); `{#anchor}` IDs on Part headings enable these URLs.
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## Modules vs notebooks {#modules-vs-notebooks}

- **Modules** (this page, 01–08): **objectives-first** site pages — concepts, links to slides/data, and **short or canonical code** (especially **`tidymodels`** on penguins in **04** — train/test + Day 2 (Tuesday) include — and **07–08**).
- **Notebooks** ([`notebooks/`](../notebooks/index.qmd)): **long IDE labs** — many chunks, heavy plots, **simulated genes**, and classical **`lm` / `glm` / `rpart` / `glmnet`** on penguins (knit locally or submit if the course asks).

**Format inventory (site spine)**

| Asset | Primary role |
|-------|----------------|
| [Home](../index.qmd) | Navigation |
| [Curriculum outline](../curriculum-outline.qmd) | Concept |
| [Day 1 (Monday) slides](../slides/day-01-monday.qmd) | Concept |
| [Day 2 (Tuesday) slides](../slides/day-02-tuesday.qmd) | Concept + **Canonical_code** (shared pipeline with Module 04) |
| [Day 4 (Thursday) slides](../slides/day-04-thursday.qmd) | Concept + code |
| [Notebook index](../notebooks/index.qmd) | **Lab_exploration** (+ homework when assigned) |

**Format inventory (modules 01–08)** — tag = main job of the page

| Module | Primary role |
|--------|----------------|
| [01](#module-01-foundations-and-generalization)–[03](#module-03-trees-and-two-cultures) | Concept |
| [04](#module-04-tidymodels-pipeline) | **Canonical_code** (train/test + full tuned pipeline; same include as Day 2 slides) |
| [05](#module-05-advanced-models)–[06](#module-06-metrics-imbalance-and-shap) | Concept |
| [07](#module-07-penguins-pick-your-metric)–[08](#module-08-penguins-compare-learners) | **Canonical_code** (metrics while tuning; compare engines) |
:::

| Module | Day focus | In plain language | Role |
|--------|-----------|-------------------|------|
| [01 — Foundations](#module-01-foundations-and-generalization) | Day 1 (Mon) | Train vs test, “too wiggly” models, one-page **data description** habit | Concept |
| [02 — Regularization](#module-02-regularization) | Day 1 (Mon) | **L1/L2**, ridge, lasso, elastic net, \(\lambda\) — with slide deep-links | Concept + penalties |
| [03 — Trees & cultures](#module-03-trees-and-two-cultures) | Day 2 (Tue) | Two ways of thinking about statistics + **decision trees** (“if–then” rules) | Concept |
| [04 — Modeling pipeline in R](#module-04-tidymodels-pipeline) | Day 2 (Tue) | **Train/test + `glm`** first, then the **same tuned-tree code as Day 2 slides** (shared include; `tidymodels`) | Canonical_code |
| [05 — Advanced models](#module-05-advanced-models) | Day 4 (Thu) | Stronger “black box” models (random forest, boosting, neural nets) — **same checking discipline**, bigger capacity | Concept |
| [06 — Metrics & explanations](#module-06-metrics-imbalance-and-shap) | Day 4 (Thu) | Picking scores that match the biology/clinic question, imbalance, and reading **SHAP** plots carefully | Concept |
| [07 — Pick your metric](#module-07-penguins-pick-your-metric) | Day 4 / lab | **Extension:** multi-metric `select_best()` (Tuesday lecture uses **accuracy** only) | Canonical_code |
| [08 — Compare learners](#module-08-penguins-compare-learners) | Day 4 / lab | Same folds + recipe, compare **random forest**, **XGBoost**, **small MLP** (`fit_resamples`) | Canonical_code |

---

## Module 01: Foundations and Generalization {#module-01-foundations-and-generalization}

**Predicting an outcome from measurements**, splitting data so you are honest about performance, and the “too simple vs too wiggly” picture you see on Day 1 (Monday). Links **gene simulations** and **penguins** so the same ideas appear on two tables.

**[Open Module 01 →](module-01-foundations.qmd)**

---

## Module 02: Regularization {#module-02-regularization}

**\(L_2\)** (ridge) vs **\(L_1\)** (lasso), **elastic net**, choosing **`lambda`**, and contrast with **AIC** — with links to [lasso](../slides/day-01-monday.html#/lasso-regression) and [elastic net](../slides/day-01-monday.html#/elastic-net) slides plus a small **`glmnet`** example.

**[Open Module 02 →](module-02-regularization.qmd)**

---

## Module 03: Trees and Two Cultures {#module-03-trees-and-two-cultures}

Two traditions in statistics (interpretable models vs flexible prediction), then **decision trees**: sequential splits, depth, and what a **tree diagram** means for penguins.

**[Open Module 03 →](module-03-trees-and-cultures.qmd)**

---

## Module 04: Modeling pipeline in R {#module-04-tidymodels-pipeline}

**[Train/test + `glm` →](module-04-pipeline.qmd#train-test-last-fit)** then the **[same tuned-tree code as Day 2 (Tuesday) slides](module-04-pipeline.qmd#canonical-pipeline-tuesday)** (shared include so slides and this page **never drift apart**).

**[Open Module 04 →](module-04-pipeline.qmd)**

---

## Module 05: Advanced models {#module-05-advanced-models}

Random forests, boosting, and neural nets as **more flexible** learners. Emphasis: keep **cross-validation and metrics** strict, and **swap the type of model** in the same overall workflow you learned on Day 2 (Tuesday).

**[Open Module 05 →](module-05-advanced-models.qmd)**

---

## Module 06: Metrics, imbalance, and SHAP {#module-06-metrics-imbalance-and-shap}

Choosing **numbers that match the decision** (miss a case vs call a false alarm), **class imbalance**, and using **SHAP** and importance plots as **descriptions** — not as proof you should intervene in a biological system.

**[Open Module 06 →](module-06-evaluation-and-interpretability.qmd)**

---

## Module 07: Pick the score you care about {#module-07-penguins-pick-your-metric}

**Lab extension** (not Tuesday’s lecture default): tune one tree while tracking **accuracy**, **kappa**, and **ROC AUC** — see **`select_best()`** change when the metric changes. Pairs with [Day 4 Part E](../slides/day-04-thursday.html#/part-e-metrics).

**[Open Module 07 →](module-07-penguins-choose-metrics.qmd)**

---

## Module 08: Compare a few strong models {#module-08-penguins-compare-learners}

Hold the **recipe and folds** fixed; swap **random forest**, **XGBoost**, and a **small neural net** — fair **`fit_resamples`** comparison on the same penguin task used in Modules 04 and 07.

**[Open Module 08 →](module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd)**
